docs.title: Stanchion
docs.text:
Framework-agnostic reliability primitives for agent pipelines.

Stanchion gives you the building blocks to make LLM agent pipelines robust: contract validation, checkpointing, retry policies, cost tracking, and execution tracing — without tying you to any particular agent framework.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install stanchion

Optional extras:

pip install stanchion[redis]       # Redis-backed checkpointing
pip install stanchion[langgraph]   # LangGraph adapter

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from pydantic import BaseModel

from stanchion import (
    CheckpointManager,
    ContractRegistry,
    ExecutionBudget,
    InMemoryStore,
    NodeContract,
    RunConfig,
    StanchionRunner,
    default_policy_map,
    stanchion_node,
)


class InputState(BaseModel):
    value: int


class OutputState(BaseModel):
    result: int


# 1. Define contracts
registry = ContractRegistry()
contract = NodeContract(node_id="double", input_schema=InputState, output_schema=OutputState)
registry.register(contract)

# 2. Decorate your node
@stanchion_node("double", contract)
async def double(state: InputState) -> dict:
    return {"result": state.value * 2}

# 3. Configure and run
store = InMemoryStore()
config = RunConfig(
    budget=ExecutionBudget.unlimited(),
    policy_map=default_policy_map(),
)
runner = StanchionRunner(registry, CheckpointManager(store), config)
result = await runner.run([double], InputState(value=5))

print(result.status)       # COMPLETED
print(result.final_state)  # result=10

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Concepts — understand the architecture and design decisions
  • API Reference — full API documentation

docs.title: Concepts
docs.text: Stanchion is built around five core primitives that work together to make agent pipelines reliable. Each can be used independently, but they compose naturally when wired through the StanchionRunner.

docs.title: Contracts
docs.text:
A contract defines the expected input and output schemas for a pipeline node using Pydantic models. Contracts catch data shape issues at node boundaries before they propagate downstream.

from pydantic import BaseModel
from stanchion import ContractRegistry, NodeContract

class Query(BaseModel):
    text: str
    max_results: int

class SearchResult(BaseModel):
    urls: list[str]
    scores: list[float]

registry = ContractRegistry()
registry.register(NodeContract(
    node_id="search",
    input_schema=Query,
    output_schema=SearchResult,
))

When validation fails, a ContractViolation is raised with the node ID, direction ("input" or "output"), the raw data, and Pydantic's error details. The runner classifies contract violations as terminal failures — no retries, because the data shape won't fix itself.

docs.title: Failure Classification
docs.text:
Not all errors are equal. Stanchion categorizes every exception into one of three classes:

Class Meaning Default behavior RECOVERABLE Transient failure (timeouts, rate limits) Retry up to 3 times with jittered backoff TERMINAL Permanent failure (bad data, logic errors) Stop immediately AMBIGUOUS Unknown — might resolve on retry Retry once with short backoff

Built-in rules handle common exceptions:

  • ContractViolation → TERMINAL
  • BudgetExceeded → RECOVERABLE
  • TimeoutError → RECOVERABLE
  • ValueError → AMBIGUOUS
  • Everything else → AMBIGUOUS

You can override these with custom classifiers — callables that inspect the exception and context, returning a FailureClass or None to defer:

from stanchion import FailureClass, RunConfig, ExecutionBudget

def classify_rate_limit(exc, ctx):
    if "rate limit" in str(exc).lower():
        return FailureClass.RECOVERABLE
    return None

config = RunConfig(
    budget=ExecutionBudget.unlimited(),
    classifiers=[classify_rate_limit],
)

Custom classifiers are checked first, in order. If none return a result, the built-in rules apply.

docs.title: Retry Policies
docs.text:
Each failure class maps to a retry policy controlling:

  • max_retries: How many times to retry before giving up
  • backoff_seconds: Maximum backoff (actual delay is jittered between 0 and this value)
  • fallback_node_id: An alternative node to route to (for future use)
from stanchion import FailureClass, FailurePolicy

custom_policies = {
    FailureClass.RECOVERABLE: FailurePolicy(max_retries=5, backoff_seconds=2.0),
    FailureClass.TERMINAL: FailurePolicy(max_retries=0),
    FailureClass.AMBIGUOUS: FailurePolicy(max_retries=2, backoff_seconds=0.5),
}

docs.title: Checkpointing
docs.text:
After each successful node execution, the output state is checkpointed. If a pipeline fails partway through, you can resume from the last checkpoint instead of re-running completed nodes:

from stanchion import RunConfig, ExecutionBudget

# First run fails at node3
config1 = RunConfig(run_id="run-123", budget=ExecutionBudget.unlimited())
result = await runner.run([node1, node2, node3], initial_state)
# result.status == PARTIAL or FAILED

# Resume from node3 — node1 and node2 are skipped, their output loaded from checkpoint
config2 = RunConfig(run_id="run-123", budget=ExecutionBudget.unlimited(), resume_from="node3")
result = await runner.run([node1, node2, node3], initial_state)
# result.status == RESUMED

Two storage backends are included:

  • InMemoryStore: For testing and short-lived pipelines. State is kept in-process.
  • RedisStore: For durable, distributed pipelines. Requires pip install stanchion[redis].

Implement the CheckpointStore protocol to add your own backend (Postgres, S3, DynamoDB, etc.).

docs.title: Cost Tracking
docs.text:
Stanchion tracks three resource dimensions across your pipeline:

  • Tokens: Total LLM tokens consumed
  • Cost (USD): Total monetary cost
  • Latency (ms): Total wall-clock time

Set budget limits on any dimension. When a limit is exceeded, BudgetExceeded is raised — classified as RECOVERABLE by default.

from stanchion import ExecutionBudget

budget = ExecutionBudget(
    max_tokens_total=10_000,
    max_cost_usd=0.50,
    max_latency_ms=30_000,
)

Nodes can report token usage by returning a tuple:

@stanchion_node("summarize", contract)
async def summarize(state: Input) -> dict:
    result = await llm.generate(state.text)
    return ({"summary": result.text}, result.usage.total_tokens)

docs.title: Execution Tracing
docs.text:
Every node execution attempt is recorded as a TraceEvent capturing:

  • Node ID, run ID, and attempt number
  • Timestamp and duration
  • Input and output state (as dicts)
  • Failure class and message (if failed)

The ExecutionTrace provides filtering, diffing, replay, and JSON serialization:

result = await runner.run(nodes, state)

# Inspect failures
for event in result.trace.failures():
    print(f"{event.node_id} attempt {event.attempt}: {event.failure_message}")

# Compare two runs
diffs = trace_a.diff(trace_b)

# Export for monitoring
json_payload = result.trace.to_json()

docs.title: Pipeline Execution
docs.text:
The StanchionRunner ties everything together. For each node in the sequence it:

  1. Validates input against the contract
  2. Executes the async node function
  3. Validates output against the contract
  4. Records resource usage and checks the budget
  5. Saves a checkpoint
  6. Records a trace event

On failure, it classifies the exception, applies the retry policy, and either retries with jittered backoff, stops with a terminal status, or gives up after exhausting retries.

Input State → [Contract] → Node → [Contract] → [Budget] → [Checkpoint] → Output State
                              ↑                                              |
                              └──────── retry (if recoverable) ──────────────┘

docs.title: CheckpointStore
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Protocol for checkpoint storage backends.

Implementations must provide save, load, and delete. Optionally implement save_typed and load_typed for schema-aware storage and retrieval.

docs.title: save(run_id, node_id, state)
docs.text: Persist the state for a given run/node pair.

docs.title: load(run_id, node_id)
docs.text: Load previously saved state, or return None if not found.

docs.title: delete(run_id)
docs.text: Delete all checkpoints for a run.

docs.title: InMemoryStore
docs.text:
In-memory checkpoint store for testing and short-lived pipelines.

State is serialized to JSON internally so that round-trip behavior matches persistent backends.

docs.title: save(run_id, node_id, state)
docs.text: Save state as JSON, recording the schema type for later deserialization.

docs.title: load(run_id, node_id)
docs.text: Load state using the schema that was recorded at save time.

docs.title: delete(run_id)
docs.text: Delete all checkpoints for a run.

docs.title: save_typed(run_id, node_id, state, schema)
docs.text: Save state coerced to a specific schema type.

docs.title: load_typed(run_id, node_id, schema)
docs.text: Load state, deserializing with the provided schema regardless of what was saved.

docs.title: RedisStore
docs.text:
Redis-backed checkpoint store for durable, distributed pipelines.

Requires the redis extra: pip install stanchion[redis].

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default redis_url str

Redis connection URL (e.g. redis://localhost:6379).

required ttl_seconds int

Time-to-live for checkpoint keys. Defaults to 3600 (1 hour).

3600

docs.title: save(run_id, node_id, state)
docs.text: Persist state to Redis with TTL.

docs.title: load(run_id, node_id)
docs.text: Load state from Redis, resolving the schema dynamically.

docs.title: delete(run_id)
docs.text: Delete all checkpoints for a run using SCAN + pipeline for efficiency.

docs.title: save_typed(run_id, node_id, state, schema)
docs.text: Persist state to Redis using an explicit schema reference.

docs.title: load_typed(run_id, node_id, schema)
docs.text: Load state from Redis, deserializing with the provided schema.

docs.title: CheckpointManager
docs.text:
High-level checkpoint operations: save, resume, and clear.

Wraps a :class:CheckpointStore and handles schema-aware loading when the underlying store supports it.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default store CheckpointStore

The storage backend to use.

required

docs.title: checkpoint(run_id, node_id, state)
docs.text: Save a checkpoint for the given run/node pair.

docs.title: resume(run_id, node_id, schema)
docs.text:
Load a checkpoint, coercing to schema if the store supports typed loading.

Returns None if no checkpoint exists.

Raises:

Type Description TypeError

If the loaded state does not match schema and the store does not support typed loading.

docs.title: clear(run_id)
docs.text: Delete all checkpoints for a run.

docs.title: NodeContract
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

Schema contract binding a node to its expected input and output types.

Attributes:

Name Type Description node_id str

Unique identifier for the node this contract governs.

input_schema type[BaseModel]

Pydantic model class that incoming state must conform to.

output_schema type[BaseModel]

Pydantic model class that outgoing state must conform to.

docs.title: ContractViolation
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised when node input or output fails schema validation.

Attributes:

Name Type Description node_id

The node whose contract was violated.

direction

Whether the violation occurred on "input" or "output".

raw

The raw dict that failed validation.

errors

List of Pydantic validation error details.

docs.title: BoundaryValidator
docs.text:
Callable that validates a raw dict against a contract's input or output schema.

Returns the validated Pydantic model on success, raises :class:ContractViolation on failure.

docs.title: __call__(contract, direction, raw)
docs.text:
Validate raw against the appropriate schema of contract.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default contract NodeContract

The node contract containing the schema.

required direction Literal['input', 'output']

"input" or "output" — selects which schema to validate against.

required raw dict[str, Any]

The dict to validate.

required

Returns:

Type Description BaseModel

A validated Pydantic model instance.

Raises:

Type Description ContractViolation

If validation fails.

docs.title: ContractRegistry
docs.text:
Registry mapping node IDs to their contracts with built-in validation.

Example::

registry = ContractRegistry()
registry.register(NodeContract(node_id="extract", input_schema=In, output_schema=Out))
validated = registry.validate_input("extract", {"url": "https://..."})

docs.title: register(contract)
docs.text: Register a contract for a node, replacing any existing contract for that node ID.

docs.title: get(node_id)
docs.text:
Return the contract for node_id.

Raises:

Type Description KeyError

If no contract is registered for node_id.

docs.title: validate_input(node_id, raw)
docs.text: Validate raw against the input schema of the contract for node_id.

docs.title: validate_output(node_id, raw)
docs.text: Validate raw against the output schema of the contract for node_id.

docs.title: ExecutionBudget
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

Optional budget constraints for a pipeline run.

All fields are optional — None means no limit on that dimension.

Attributes:

Name Type Description max_tokens_total int | None

Maximum total tokens across all nodes.

max_cost_usd float | None

Maximum total cost in USD across all nodes.

max_latency_ms int | None

Maximum total latency in milliseconds across all nodes.

docs.title: unlimited() classmethod
docs.text: Return a budget with no constraints on any dimension.

docs.title: NodeUsage dataclass
docs.text:
Observed resource usage for a single node execution.

Attributes:

Name Type Description node_id str

The node that was executed.

tokens_used int

Number of tokens consumed.

cost_usd float

Cost in USD for this execution.

latency_ms int

Wall-clock duration in milliseconds.

docs.title: CostTracker
docs.text:
Accumulates per-node resource usage and checks it against budgets.

Usage is keyed by node_id and accumulated across multiple calls to :meth:record for the same node.

docs.title: total_tokens property
docs.text: Total tokens consumed across all nodes.

docs.title: total_cost_usd property
docs.text: Total cost in USD across all nodes.

docs.title: total_latency_ms property
docs.text: Total latency in milliseconds across all nodes.

docs.title: record(usage)
docs.text: Record a node execution's resource usage, accumulating with any prior usage for that node.

docs.title: check_budget(budget, node_id)
docs.text:
Check aggregate usage across all nodes against the budget.

Raises:

Type Description BudgetExceeded

If any budget dimension is exceeded.

docs.title: summary()
docs.text: Return a copy of the per-node usage map.

docs.title: BudgetExceeded
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised when cumulative resource usage exceeds a budget limit.

Attributes:

Name Type Description dimension

Which budget dimension was exceeded ("tokens", "cost", or "latency").

limit

The budget cap that was exceeded.

actual

The actual cumulative value that triggered the violation.

node_id

The node whose execution caused the budget to be exceeded.

docs.title: ModelHint dataclass
docs.text:
Hint for model selection on a per-node basis.

Attributes:

Name Type Description node_id str

Node this hint applies to.

preferred_model str

Primary model identifier to use.

fallback_model str | None

Model to fall back to if the preferred model is unavailable.

max_tokens int | None

Optional token limit for this node's LLM call.

docs.title: FailureClass
docs.text:
Bases: StrEnum

Classification of a pipeline failure for deciding retry behavior.

  • RECOVERABLE: The failure is transient and the node should be retried.
  • TERMINAL: The failure is permanent and the pipeline should stop.
  • AMBIGUOUS: The failure may or may not be recoverable; limited retries are attempted.

docs.title: FailurePolicy
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

Retry behavior for a given failure class.

Attributes:

Name Type Description max_retries int

Maximum number of retry attempts before giving up.

backoff_seconds float

Maximum backoff duration in seconds (actual delay is jittered).

fallback_node_id str | None

Optional node to route to instead of retrying.

docs.title: NodeContext dataclass
docs.text:
Context passed to failure classifiers for making classification decisions.

Attributes:

Name Type Description node_id str

The node that raised the exception.

attempt int

The attempt number (1-based) when the failure occurred.

run_id str

The pipeline run identifier.

docs.title: classify(exc, context, classifiers=None)
docs.text:
Determine the failure class of an exception.

Custom classifiers are checked first in order. If none return a result, built-in rules apply:

  • :class:~stanchion.contracts.ContractViolation → TERMINAL
  • :class:~stanchion.cost.BudgetExceeded → RECOVERABLE
  • :class:TimeoutError → RECOVERABLE
  • :class:ValueError → AMBIGUOUS
  • Everything else → AMBIGUOUS

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default exc Exception

The exception to classify.

required context NodeContext

Execution context at the time of failure.

required classifiers list[Classifier] | None

Optional list of custom classifiers to check first.

None

Returns:

Type Description FailureClass

The determined failure class.

docs.title: RetryBudget
docs.text: Tracks retry counts per (run_id, node_id) pair and enforces limits.

docs.title: increment(run_id, node_id)
docs.text: Increment and return the retry count for the given run/node pair.

docs.title: exhausted(run_id, node_id, policy)
docs.text: Return True if the retry count has reached the policy's max_retries.

docs.title: LangGraphAdapter
docs.text:
Wraps a LangGraph graph to apply stanchion contract validation on each node.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default registry ContractRegistry

Contract registry for validation.

required runner Any

The stanchion runner instance (for future use with cost/trace integration).

required

docs.title: wrap(graph)
docs.text: Discover stanchion-decorated nodes in graph and replace them with validating wrappers.

docs.title: stanchion_langgraph_node(node_id, contract)
docs.text:
Decorator that attaches stanchion contract metadata to a LangGraph node function.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default node_id str

Unique identifier for this node.

required contract NodeContract

The input/output contract for this node.

required

docs.title: extract_state_dict(state)
docs.text:
Convert a LangGraph state object to a plain dict.

Supports dicts, Pydantic models, objects with a .dict() method, and plain objects with __dict__.

Raises:

Type Description TypeError

If the state cannot be converted to a dict.

docs.title: RunConfig
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

Configuration for a single pipeline run.

Attributes:

Name Type Description run_id str

Unique identifier for this run. Auto-generated if not provided.

budget ExecutionBudget

Resource budget constraints for the run.

policy_map PolicyMap

Mapping from failure class to retry policy.

model_hints dict[str, ModelHint]

Per-node model selection hints.

classifiers list[Classifier]

Custom failure classifiers checked before built-in rules.

resume_from str | None

Node ID to resume execution from (skips earlier nodes).

docs.title: StanchionRunner
docs.text:
Orchestrates sequential node execution with reliability primitives.

Runs a sequence of async node functions, applying contract validation, retry policies, cost tracking, and checkpointing at each step.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default registry ContractRegistry

Contract registry for input/output validation.

required checkpoint_manager CheckpointManager

Manager for saving and loading checkpoints.

required config RunConfig

Run configuration including budget, policies, and classifiers.

required

Example::

runner = StanchionRunner(registry, checkpoint_manager, config)
result = await runner.run([node1, node2], initial_state)
print(result.status, result.final_state)

docs.title: run(node_sequence, initial_state) async
docs.text:
Execute a sequence of nodes with full reliability instrumentation.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default node_sequence list[NodeFunc]

Ordered list of async node functions to execute.

required initial_state BaseModel

The initial Pydantic model state to pass to the first node.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description An ExecutionResult

class:~stanchion.trace.ExecutionResult with final state, trace, and cost info.

docs.title: stanchion_node(node_id, contract)
docs.text:
Decorator that attaches contract metadata to an async node function.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default node_id str

Unique identifier for this node.

required contract NodeContract

The input/output contract for this node.

required

Returns:

Type Description Callable[..., Any]

A decorator that marks the function as a stanchion pipeline node.

Example::

@stanchion_node("extract", NodeContract(node_id="extract", input_schema=In, output_schema=Out))
async def extract(state: In) -> dict:
    return {"url": state.url, "content": "..."}

docs.title: TraceEvent dataclass
docs.text:
A single recorded execution attempt for a node.

Attributes:

Name Type Description node_id str

The node that was executed.

run_id str

The pipeline run this event belongs to.

attempt int

The attempt number (1-based).

timestamp_utc datetime

When the attempt started.

input_state dict[str, Any]

The input dict passed to the node.

output_state dict[str, Any] | None

The output dict returned, or None on failure.

duration_ms int

Wall-clock duration in milliseconds.

failure FailureClass | None

The failure classification, or None on success.

failure_message str | None

Human-readable failure description, or None on success.

docs.title: ExecutionTrace
docs.text:
Ordered collection of :class:TraceEvent instances for a pipeline run.

Provides filtering, diffing, serialization, and iteration over events.

docs.title: append(event)
docs.text: Append a trace event.

docs.title: events_for(node_id)
docs.text: Return all events for the given node.

docs.title: failures()
docs.text: Return all events that represent failures.

docs.title: to_json()
docs.text: Serialize all events to a JSON string.

docs.title: diff(other)
docs.text:
Compare this trace against other, ignoring timestamps and durations.

Returns a list of human-readable difference descriptions. An empty list means the traces are logically equivalent.

docs.title: replay()
docs.text: Yield events in the order they were recorded.

docs.title: RunStatus
docs.text:
Bases: StrEnum

Terminal status of a pipeline run.

  • COMPLETED: All nodes executed successfully.
  • FAILED: A terminal failure stopped the pipeline.
  • PARTIAL: Retries were exhausted on a recoverable/ambiguous failure.
  • RESUMED: The pipeline completed after resuming from a checkpoint.

docs.title: ExecutionResult dataclass
docs.text:
Final output of a pipeline run.

Attributes:

Name Type Description run_id str

The unique identifier for this run.

status RunStatus

Terminal status of the run.

final_state BaseModel | None

The last valid state produced, or the initial state on early failure.

trace ExecutionTrace

Complete execution trace with all attempts.

total_cost_usd float

Cumulative cost in USD across all nodes.

total_tokens int

Cumulative token usage across all nodes.
